whatThisIs: Look up an NDC in the bundled FDA + NLM RxNorm crosswalk and return the corresponding RxNorm concepts - typically the ingredient (IN), the semantic clinical drug component (SCDC), and the semantic clinical drug (SCD), which together describe the product as drug name + strength + dose form. Sophie Well accepts either the 10-digit FDA NDC or the 11-digit CMS billing form, normalizes leading zeros, and shows the resolved RxNorm CUI(s) plus the human-readable concept strings.
whenToUse: Use this when you have a specific package (an NDC on a bottle, a label image, or a payer remittance line) and you need to know the drug it represents in a way that is comparable across product variants - generic vs. brand, 50 mg tablet vs. 100 mg tablet, oral solution vs. tablet. The RxNorm graph is the bridge: ingredient-level questions ("is this an NSAID?", "is this on the patient's allergy list?") answer at the IN level; formulary and substitution questions answer at the SCD level. The crosswalk shipped here reflects a specific RxNorm release; for the most current ingredient terms re-pull RxNorm before publishing decisions.
